description: Mixed synthetic conversation cases: interruptions, backchannels, and normal turn endings.

cases.title: Deadline correction
cases.description: Speaker B corrects a deadline detail while Speaker A is finishing the setup.

speaker_a.text: I think the design review is on Thursday afternoon, so we should send the notes by...
speaker_a.instruction: A grounded adult male project lead speaking naturally in a small team meeting. Use a steady conversational pace. Near the end, leave the phrase slightly unfinished with a modest incomplete cadence, but do not slow down dramatically or sound theatrical.

speaker_b.instruction: A calm adult male colleague entering quickly with a concise correction. Speak clearly, lightly, and confidently, without sounding annoyed.

cases.alignment_notes: Try starting B 100 to 250 ms before A ends.

cases.title: Collaborative completion
cases.description: Speaker B recognizes what Speaker A is referring to and completes the idea.

speaker_a.text: The thing that finally fixed the import bug was changing that one setting in the...
speaker_a.instruction: A relaxed adult male software engineer explaining a recent debugging result to a teammate. Sound casual and engaged. End with a light incomplete cadence, as if the other person may know the word, but keep the delivery subtle.

speaker_b.instruction: A friendly adult male teammate entering with quick recognition. Speak naturally and a little brighter than neutral, as a collaborative completion rather than an interruption for control.

cases.alignment_notes: Try starting B around 50 to 200 ms before A ends.

cases.title: Quiet schedule backchannel
cases.description: Speaker B gives a low-prominence acknowledgment while Speaker A continues speaking.

speaker_a.text: After the morning call, I moved the budget review to next week, because finance still needs one more day to check the numbers.
speaker_a.instruction: A natural adult male speaker explaining a scheduling update at a moderate pace. Continue smoothly through the whole sentence. Include a small prosodic boundary after 'morning call' but do not yield the floor.

speaker_b.instruction: A very short, quiet adult male listener acknowledgment. Keep it low in prominence, brief, and supportive, without sounding like an attempt to take the floor.

cases.alignment_notes: Try placing B near the boundary after 'morning call'.

cases.title: Short confirmation backchannel
cases.description: Speaker B gives a minimal confirmation during an ongoing explanation.

speaker_a.text: I checked the logs from last night, and the restart happened right after the backup job finished, so the timing makes sense.
speaker_a.instruction: A calm adult male engineer summarizing evidence in a normal conversational voice. Maintain the same turn throughout. Add a small phrase boundary after 'last night' but continue without pausing too long.

speaker_b.instruction: A soft, brief adult male backchannel that means 'I am following.' Keep it quiet, quick, and non-interrupting.

cases.alignment_notes: Try placing B after 'last night' or during the following short boundary.

cases.title: Normal handoff question
cases.description: Speaker A completes a turn naturally, then Speaker B asks a follow-up question after a short gap.

speaker_a.text: That should give us enough time to review the numbers before the client call.
speaker_a.instruction: A composed adult male voice finishing a practical workplace comment. Use a natural complete sentence ending with final intonation, not a trail off.

speaker_b.text: Do you want me to update the deck after that?
speaker_b.instruction: A helpful adult male colleague asking a straightforward follow-up question. Begin naturally after the previous speaker finishes, with a small conversational pause.

cases.alignment_notes: Try starting B 150 to 500 ms after A ends.

cases.title: Normal handoff response
cases.description: Speaker A finishes a casual update, then Speaker B responds without overlap.

speaker_a.text: I dropped the package at reception, so it should be ready for pickup this afternoon.
speaker_a.instruction: A natural adult male voice giving a simple update. Speak at a relaxed conversational pace and finish with a complete, ordinary turn ending.

speaker_b.text: Great, I will grab it after lunch.
speaker_b.instruction: A relaxed adult male voice responding after the other speaker has clearly finished. Sound appreciative and matter-of-fact, with normal conversational timing.

cases.alignment_notes: Try starting B 200 to 600 ms after A ends.
